mission.title: prometheus: Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody
mission.description: Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody. Requested by 53+ users.

steps.title: Check current prometheus deployment
steps.description:
Verify your prometheus version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
helm list -n prometheus 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working prometheus installation.

steps.title: Review prometheus configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant prometheus configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get configmap -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=prometheus
```
With the update to 2.0, the Docker container was modified so that Prometheus runs as the user "nobody" (UID/GID 99/99) (#2859). While it's good that Prometheus is no longer running as root, it's not the best idea either to run as "nobody".

steps.title: Apply the fix for Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody
steps.description:
I re-wrote the `Dockerfile` to be more in line with common `Dockerfile`s.

The only significant difference is the user running promgen: I removed the promgen user, that means that the web app and the worker are now run as root. This simplifies the permissions required for the configuration files.
```yaml
$ docker run --user 1234:5678 --rm -ti busybox
/ $ id
uid=1234 gid=5678
/ $
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus
kubectl get events -n prometheus --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I re-wrote the `Dockerfile` to be more in line with common `Dockerfile`s.

The only significant difference is the user running promgen: I removed the promgen user, that means that the web app and the worker are now run as root. This simplifies the permissions required for the configuration files. Running applications in docker as root is a common strategy.
codeSnippets:
$ docker run --user 1234:5678 --rm -ti busybox
/ $ id
uid=1234 gid=5678
/ $

$ docker run -it --rm --entrypoint sh prom/prometheus:v2.0.0

/prometheus $ id
uid=65534(nobody) gid=65534(nogroup)
Can see the user in the container with

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible.
